items.headline: City updates rules for affordable housing as subsidies expire
items.summary: The Council approved new affordability restrictions to protect housing units when project-based subsidies end or properties face foreclosure. This affects how long affordable apartments stay affordable in San José.

items.headline: Storm sewer system gets major city-wide upgrade plan
items.summary: The City approved a master planning agreement to overhaul San José's storm sewer infrastructure. This addresses drainage and flooding issues across the entire city.

items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital improvement spending plans
items.summary: The council held a study session to examine Mountain View's planned infrastructure and facility projects for fiscal year 2026-27. This is where residents can learn what physical improvements or construction projects the city is prioritizing with public funds.

items.headline: New rules proposed for tenant protections in Sunnyvale
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend the Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect rental agreements and tenant rights across the city.

items.headline: City releases youth services and library facility improvement plans
items.summary: The Council reviewed status reports on children and youth programs, including the Safe Summer Initiative, plus a new Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. These outline how the city will invest in youth services and upgrade library locations.

items.headline: Downtown historic building may get expanded floor area
items.summary: A request to elevate 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status would allow the owner to build an additional 5,000 square feet as an incentive for historic and seismic rehabilitation. This could materially change the building's size and the streetscape in downtown Palo Alto.

items.headline: City of Cupertino launches summer jobs program for youth
items.summary: The city presented details on a new Summer Jobs initiative on April 8. This program offers employment opportunities for young residents during the summer months.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents a significant change to the character of that commercial area and will require City Council approval.
